skill.name: ai-podcast-creation
skill.description: Create podcast scripts, narration, multi-voice conversations, background music, and merged audio through inference.sh command examples.
skill.summary: Build AI podcast episodes with TTS voices, generated music, and audio merging workflows.
skill.author: inference-sh

security_audit.summary: The static pipe-to-shell finding is a true positive: SKILL.md documents running a remote installer through sh. The many command findings are mostly legitimate inference.sh CLI examples, but they execute external services and can transmit user scripts or documents. The weak cryptography alerts appear to be false positives; I found no cryptographic operations in the reviewed file.

high_findings.title: Remote Installer Piped Directly To Shell
high_findings.description: SKILL.md instructs users to download a script from inference.sh and execute it with sh in one command. This bypasses review of the installer and can execute arbitrary remote code if the endpoint, network path, or installer is compromised.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicitly shown as a curl download piped into sh. This is a well-known remote code execution pattern, although I found no evidence of malicious intent in the file.

medium_findings.title: External AI Services Receive User Content
medium_findings.description: The workflow sends podcast text, document excerpts, prompts, and generated assets to inference.sh apps and OpenRouter Claude models. This may be legitimate, but users need clear consent and data handling expectations before sending unpublished or sensitive content.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples clearly call external inference apps with user-provided prompts and document content. This is expected for the skill purpose, so the concern is disclosure and consent rather than confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Service URL
low_findings.description: The skill links to inference.sh and uses it as the command source. This is expected for an inference.sh skill, but it creates a dependency on a third-party endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is visible and directly tied to the skill provider. It is not hidden or obfuscated, but the endpoint still affects user trust and supply-chain risk.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static scanner reported weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but the referenced lines contain metadata, capability text, voice tables, and podcast best practices. No evidence of cryptographic hashing, encryption, or weak algorithm use was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the flagged lines shows ordinary prose and Markdown table content. I found no code or instructions that perform cryptographic operations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Pipe To Shell Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The quick start uses a curl download piped to sh. Users should install through a verifiable package manager or inspect the script before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact pattern appears in the documented command. The risk is inherent to executing remote installer content without inspection.

content.value_statement: Podcast production often requires separate scripting, voice, music, and editing tools. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured workflows for creating AI podcast segments with inference.sh apps.
actual_capabilities: Generate single-voice podcast narration with Kokoro TTS examples.
Create multi-voice host and guest conversations with separate voice settings.
Draft scripts with Claude through OpenRouter command examples.
Generate intro, outro, background, and transition music prompts.
Merge audio clips with crossfades and optional background music.
Convert document content into a conversational podcast format.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI and account setup before most workflows can run.
Audio quality depends on the selected model, voice, input text, and service availability.
The skill provides command workflows, not a local audio editor interface.
Sensitive scripts or documents may be sent to external AI services.

use_cases.title: Produce A Branded Podcast Segment
use_cases.description: Create a narrated segment with a consistent voice, intro music, and smooth transitions.

use_cases.title: Turn Documents Into Audio Discussions
use_cases.description: Transform notes, reports, or articles into a two-host conversation for easier review.

use_cases.title: Build Audio Newsletters
use_cases.description: Generate recurring voice content with news summaries, analysis sections, and outro music.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Short Narration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 90-second podcast narration about [topic]. Use a clear introduction, three key points, and a concise closing.

prompt_templates.title: Write A Two-Host Script
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a five-minute podcast script about [topic] for HOST_A and HOST_B. Mark each speaker clearly and keep the tone conversational.

prompt_templates.title: Design A Full Episode Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a full AI podcast episode for [audience]. Include script sections, voice choices, intro music, outro music, and merge order.

prompt_templates.title: Convert A Document Into Audio
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this document into a podcast discussion. Extract the main points, write a two-host script, and recommend voice and music settings: [document text].

output_examples.input: Create a short product update podcast about a new analytics dashboard.
output_examples.output: A 90-second narration outline with an opening hook, three update points, a closing message, and a recommended professional voice.

output_examples.input: Make a two-host discussion from a research memo.
output_examples.output: A structured dialogue where one host explains the memo and the other asks clarifying questions for listeners.

output_examples.input: Add music and transitions to a finished script.
output_examples.output: A production plan with intro music, transition sounds, background volume guidance, and final merge order.

best_practices: Review scripts before audio generation to remove errors and sensitive information.
Use distinct voices for different speakers and keep segments short.
Keep background music low so speech remains clear.
anti_patterns: Do not pipe remote installers into a shell without inspecting the script first.
Do not submit confidential documents unless external processing is approved.
Do not rely on generated music or voices without checking licensing and usage terms.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It helps create podcast scripts, TTS narration, multi-voice conversations, music prompts, and merged audio workflows.

faq.question: Does it require inference.sh?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented workflows rely on the inference.sh CLI and hosted inference apps.

faq.question: Can it create multi-speaker episodes?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes examples for host and guest voices, interleaved segments, and merged conversations.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: What should I check before publishing audio?
faq.answer: Review the script, generated voice output, music licensing, factual claims, and consent for any source material.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with private documents?
faq.answer: Use caution. The workflows may send document text and prompts to external AI services.
